rules.description: Confirm uses a trusted backend actor session token; missing or expired sessions are authentication failures, not business policy blockers.
Malformed, expired, or otherwise invalid actor tokens are authentication failures and must not be reported as business policy blockers.
Confirm payload keys must be canonical field ids; localized labels are display-only and are rejected as malformed input.
AI may prepare, explain, summarize, and draft, but must never confirm a card when forbiddenForAi is true.
The actor role must satisfy the card confirmation requiredRole. Manager may confirm operator cards; admin may confirm all cards.
Contract-only slices may prepare cards but cannot confirm them until runtime status is upgraded.
Business blockers that are not auth, role, AI, slice capability, or idempotency failures use a stable business rule violation class.
Duplicate idempotency keys return the existing durable result; conflicting reuse of a key is rejected without side effects.
Accommodation money ledgers reject non-cash writes without evidence and validate refund, deduction, payment, and allocation commands against backend ledger state.
Checkout and service slices may request settlement or release only after the required upstream runtime facts are confirmed.
Period analytics keeps deposit liability separate from revenue and expense, and closes a period only after metrics, finance, operations, and high-risk action plan requirements are met.
